dimension: Demo app swaps-api page — the FRONTEND reference flow (API-only + sign/broadcast via wallet). Files live on branch origin/feat/swaps-api-v2.

files_read.role: Route page shell. Holds the orders[] state, renders one <OrderStatus> per order plus a single <SwapCard setOrders={...}>. This is the entire 'page' — all logic lives in SwapCard.

The whole feature flow in one component: token map load, chain/token selectors, debounced quote, deadline, allowance, approve (sign+broadcast), create-intent (sign+broadcast), submit-tx, plus per-chain client-side prereqs (Stellar trustline, NEAR storage, Bitcoin Bound trading wallet). Pushes resulting tx hashes into setOrders.
Polls swap status via useSwapsApiSubmitTxStatus (1s interval, stops on executed|failed) and renders status / dstIntentTxHash / intent_hash / failedAtStep / failureReason / userMessage.
Reusable shadcn Select wrapper over a string[] chainList; chainDisplayName() maps chain key -> human name via chainIdToChainName with a try/catch passthrough fallback.

Per-chain sign+broadcast dispatcher for the unsigned RawTxReturnType the API returns. Exports signAndBroadcastSwapsApiTx(), isSignableSwapsApiChain(), waitForTxFinality(), and the SwapsApiSignError class. The CENTRAL frontend mechanism: API returns raw tx, this signs+broadcasts via wallet provider.

Two pure converters bridging API JSON types to SDK/wallet types: toIntentRequest(IntentResponseV2 decimal-strings -> IntentRequestV2 bigints) and toXToken(SwapTokenV2 plain-string fields -> branded XToken).

Exports SWAPS_API_CONFIG = { baseURL: 'https://canary-api.sodax.com/v1' } as const satisfies RequestOverrideConfig — passed per-call as apiConfig to every useSwapsApi* hook to target canary without changing app-wide SDK config.
Generic useDebouncedValue<T>(value, delayMs=400) hook; debounces the amount input that feeds the quote request.
Route registration (diff): adds `import SwapsApiPage from './pages/swaps-api/page'` and a router child `{ path: '/swaps-api', element: <SwapsApiPage /> }`.
Nav registration (diff): adds `{ to: '/swaps-api', label: 'Swaps API' }` to the navLinks array (sits between Solver and Bridge).
Baseline existing bridge demo page (working tree). Just renders <BridgeManager/>; uses the in-SDK BridgeService hooks, NOT the API pattern. This is what the new bridge-api page will sit alongside.

key_findings: PAGE SHELL (page.tsx): `const [orders, setOrders] = useState<SwapsApiOrder[]>([])`; renders `orders.map((order,index) => <OrderStatus key={`${order.txHash}-${index}`} order={order} />)` then `<SwapCard setOrders={setOrders} />`. SwapCard prop type: `{ setOrders: (value: SetStateAction<SwapsApiOrder[]>) => void }`.

SwapsApiOrder type (OrderStatus.tsx): `{ txHash: string; srcChainKey: string; apiBaseURL: string }`. Order objects are created in SwapCard.handleSwap as `{ txHash: spokeTxHash, srcChainKey: src.chain, apiBaseURL: SWAPS_API_CONFIG.baseURL }`.

API CONFIG PATTERN: every hook call receives `apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG` inside its `params` object. SWAPS_API_CONFIG is `{ baseURL: 'https://canary-api.sodax.com/v1' } as const satisfies RequestOverrideConfig` (type RequestOverrideConfig imported from '@sodax/dapp-kit'). This is the ONLY way the canary endpoint is selected — no app-wide config change.

ALL dapp-kit hooks imported in SwapCard from '@sodax/dapp-kit': loadRadfiSession, useSodaxContext, useNearStorageGate, useRequestTrustline, useStellarTrustlineCheck, useSwapsApiAllowance, useSwapsApiApprove, useSwapsApiCreateIntent, useSwapsApiDeadline, useSwapsApiQuote, useSwapsApiSubmitTx, useSwapsApiTokens, useTradingWalletBalance, useXBalances, ChainKeys.

WALLET-LAYER hooks imported from '@sodax/wallet-sdk-react': getXChainType, useEvmSwitchChain, useWalletProvider, useXAccount, useXConnection, useXDisconnect, useXService. OrderStatus additionally imports useSwapsApiSubmitTxStatus from '@sodax/dapp-kit'.

STEP 1 TOKENS: `const { data: tokensByChain } = useSwapsApiTokens({ params: { apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG } })`. tokensByChain is a record keyed by chain-key string -> SwapTokenV2[]. `chainList = useMemo(() => Object.keys(tokensByChain ?? {}), [tokensByChain])`. A useEffect seeds default src/dst tokens (`tokensByChain[chain]?.[0]`) once the map arrives. Chain keys are plain strings; cast to SpokeChainKey for wallet hooks.

STEP 2 SELECTORS: src/dst state shape `{ chain: string; token: SwapTokenV2 | undefined }`, defaults chain=ChainKeys.ARBITRUM_MAINNET / ChainKeys.POLYGON_MAINNET. Chain pickers use <SelectChain chainList value setChain placeholder id label/>. Token pickers are inline shadcn <Select> over tokensByChain[chain]. onChangeDirection swaps src<->dst. Amount Input (type=number) -> setSourceAmount.

STEP 3 BALANCES (wallet-layer, not API): `useXBalances({ params: { xService: useXService({xChainType: getXChainType(srcChainKey)}), xChainId: srcChainKey, xTokens: src.token ? [toXToken(src.token)] : [], address: sourceAccount.address } })`. Note tokens must be converted via toXToken() so wallet hooks accept API-sourced tokens. `sourceTokenBalance = sourceBalances?.[src.token?.address ?? ''] ?? 0n`.

STEP 4 QUOTE: amount debounced via `useDebouncedValue(sourceAmount)` (400ms). quoteBody is a `QuoteRequestV2 | undefined` memo, undefined when tokens missing or Number(amount)<=0; shape: `{ tokenSrc: src.token.address, tokenSrcChainKey: src.chain, tokenDst: dst.token.address, tokenDstChainKey: dst.chain, amount: parseUnits(debouncedAmount, src.token.decimals).toString(), quoteType: 'exact_input' }`. `const quoteQuery = useSwapsApiQuote({ params: { body: quoteBody, apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG } }); const quote = quoteQuery.data`. quote.quotedAmount is a decimal string in raw units.

minOutputAmount memo: `new BigNumber(quote.quotedAmount).multipliedBy(new BigNumber(100).minus(new BigNumber(slippage))).div(100).toFixed(0)` (raw-units decimal string). slippage default '0.5'. exchangeRate via calculateExchangeRate(BigNumber(amount), BigNumber(formatUnits(BigInt(quote.quotedAmount), dst.decimals))).

STEP 5 DEADLINE: `const deadlineQuery = useSwapsApiDeadline({ params: { query: { offsetSeconds: 300 }, apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG } })`. buildIntentParams() calls `await deadlineQuery.refetch()` to anchor a fresh deadline to the hub clock, fallback `String(Math.floor(Date.now()/1000)+300)`.

STEP 5 BUILD INTENT: buildIntentParams() (fired by the Swap dialog trigger) validates quote/tokens/accounts, resolves dstAddress (Bitcoin dest -> loadRadfiSession(destAccount.address)?.tradingAddress, blocks if missing), then setIntentParams({ srcChainKey: src.chain, dstChainKey: dst.chain, inputToken: src.token.address, outputToken: dst.token.address, inputAmount: parseUnits(debouncedAmount, src.token.decimals).toString(), minOutputAmount, deadline, allowPartialFill: false, srcAddress: sourceAccount.address, dstAddress }) — type CreateIntentParamsV2.

STEP 6 ALLOWANCE: `const { data: allowance, isLoading: isAllowanceLoading, refetch: refetchAllowance } = useSwapsApiAllowance({ params: { body: intentParams, apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG } })`. Self-disabled until intentParams exists (body undefined). `hasAllowed = allowance?.valid === true`.

MUTATION HOOKS use mutateAsyncSafe (Result-style, NOT throwing): `const { mutateAsyncSafe: approve } = useSwapsApiApprove(); const { mutateAsyncSafe: createIntent } = useSwapsApiCreateIntent(); const { mutateAsyncSafe: submitTx } = useSwapsApiSubmitTx();`. Each returns a Result with `.ok` boolean, `.value` on success, `.error` on failure. Errors surfaced via formatMutationFailureMessage(result.error, 'fallback msg').

STEP 7 APPROVE (handleApprove): `const result = await approve({ body: intentParams, apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG })`; if !result.ok -> setApproveError(formatMutationFailureMessage(...)). On ok: `const txHash = await signAndBroadcastSwapsApiTx({ chainKey: srcChainKey, tx: result.value.tx, walletProvider: sourceWalletProvider })`; then `await waitForTxFinality(srcChainKey, sourceWalletProvider, txHash)`; then `await refetchAllowance()` MANUALLY (the approve hook can't invalidate the allowance query because confirmation happens client-side outside the hook). Guards isApproving state.

STEP 8 SWAP (handleSwap), the core API+sign+submit sequence: (1) `const created = await createIntent({ body: intentParams, apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG })` -> destructure `const { tx, intent, relayData } = created.value`. (2) sign+broadcast: if getXChainType(srcChainKey)==='BITCOIN' route via SDK `sodax.spoke.getSpokeService(ChainKeys.BITCOIN_MAINNET).signAndSubmitRawTransaction({ rawTx: tx as BitcoinRawTransaction, walletProvider, relayData: { address: relayData.address as Hex, payload: relayData.payload as Hex }, accessToken: session.accessToken })`; else `spokeTxHash = await signAndBroadcastSwapsApiTx({ chainKey: srcChainKey, tx, walletProvider: sourceWalletProvider })`.

STEP 8 SUBMIT-TX (the 'hand tx back to API for relay + solver' step): builds `const request: SubmitTxRequestV2 = { txHash: spokeTxHash, srcChainKey: src.chain, walletAddress: sourceAccount.address, intent: toIntentRequest(intent), relayData: relayData.payload }`; `const submitted = await submitTx({ request, apiConfig: SWAPS_API_CONFIG })`; if !submitted.ok -> setSwapError. On success: `setOrders(prev => [...prev, { txHash: spokeTxHash, srcChainKey: src.chain, apiBaseURL: SWAPS_API_CONFIG.baseURL }])` and `setOpen(false)`. Note: submitTx takes `request` (not `body`) — different param key from approve/createIntent which take `body`.

STEP 9 POLL STATUS (OrderStatus.tsx): `const apiConfig = useMemo(() => ({ baseURL: order.apiBaseURL }), [order.apiBaseURL]); const { data: statusResponse } = useSwapsApiSubmitTxStatus({ params: { txHash: order.txHash, srcChainKey: order.srcChainKey, apiConfig } })`. Hook polls /swaps/submit-tx/status every second, stops on 'executed'|'failed'. Reads `statusResponse.data` -> { status, result, failedAtStep, failureReason, userMessage }; on executed shows result.dstIntentTxHash and result.intent_hash; on failed shows failedAtStep/failureReason in red.

signAndBroadcast.ts SIGNATURE: `signAndBroadcastSwapsApiTx(args: { chainKey: SpokeChainKey; tx: RawTxReturnType; walletProvider: IWalletProvider }): Promise<string>` returns spoke tx hash/signature/digest. Dispatches on `getXChainType(chainKey)`: EVM -> `(wp as IEvmWalletProvider).sendTransaction(tx as EvmRawTransaction)`; SUI -> `(wp as ISuiWalletProvider).signAndExecuteTxn({ toJSON: async () => data })` where `const { data } = tx as SuiRawTransaction`; ICON -> `(wp as IIconWalletProvider).sendTransaction(tx as unknown as IcxCallTransaction)`; NEAR -> `(wp as INearWalletProvider).signAndSubmitTxn(tx as NearRawTransaction)`; SOLANA -> `signAndSendTransaction(tx as TxReturnType<SolanaChainKey,true>)` (guarded, throws SwapsApiSignError if method absent); STELLAR/STACKS/INJECTIVE -> `signAndSendTransaction(tx as ...RawTransaction)` (each guarded); BITCOIN -> throws SwapsApiSignError (must use BitcoinSpokeService); default -> throws.

signAndBroadcast.ts SwapsApiSignError class: `constructor(readonly chainKey: string, readonly reason: 'sdk-gap'|'malformed-payload', message: string, readonly payload?: unknown)`, sets this.name='SwapsApiSignError'.

isSignableSwapsApiChain(chainKey: SpokeChainKey): boolean returns true for chainType in {EVM, SUI, ICON, NEAR, SOLANA, STELLAR, STACKS, INJECTIVE} — explicitly NOT BITCOIN. In SwapCard: `const isSourceSignable = isSignableSwapsApiChain(srcChainKey) || getXChainType(srcChainKey) === 'BITCOIN'` (Bitcoin added because it routes through the Bound path).

waitForTxFinality(chainKey, walletProvider, txHash): Promise<void> — EVM -> `(wp as IEvmWalletProvider).waitForTransactionReceipt(txHash as Hex)`; ICON -> same; SOLANA -> `waitForConfirmation(txHash, 'confirmed')`; STELLAR -> `waitForTransactionReceipt(txHash)`; SUI/NEAR/INJECTIVE -> no-op (their submit calls already resolve on execution).

mappers.ts toIntentRequest(intent: IntentResponseV2): IntentRequestV2 — spreads intent then BigInt()-converts: intentId, inputAmount, minOutputAmount, deadline, srcChain, dstChain. Needed because createIntent returns decimal-string numerics (IntentResponseV2) but submitTx needs bigint numerics (IntentRequestV2); SDK ships both types but no converter.

mappers.ts toXToken(token: SwapTokenV2): XToken — spreads token then casts chainKey as ChainKey, hubAsset as Address, vault as Address (re-brands plain-string JSON fields so wallet-layer hooks like useXBalances accept API tokens).

SelectChain props: `{ chainList: string[]; value: string; setChain: (value:string)=>void; placeholder?: string; id?: string; label?: string }`. Renders Label + shadcn Select/SelectTrigger/SelectValue/SelectContent/SelectItem. chainDisplayName(chain) = try chainIdToChainName(chain as SpokeChainKey) ?? chain; catch -> chain.

CLIENT-SIDE PREREQS layered on top of the API flow (these are chain-specific, NOT part of the generic API+sign+submit pattern): Stellar trustline via useStellarTrustlineCheck + useRequestTrustline (handleRequestTrustline); NEAR storage via useNearStorageGate (handleRegisterNearStorage, exposes needsRegistration/blocksAction/isChecking/isRegistering); Bitcoin Bound Exchange trading wallet via loadRadfiSession + useTradingWalletBalance + <BitcoinSetupPanel> + sodax.spoke BitcoinSpokeService. EVM wrong-chain via useEvmSwitchChain ({ isWrongChain, handleSwitchChain }).

UI: shadcn Card/CardHeader/CardContent/CardFooter + a Dialog (open state) that shows the intent details and the action buttons. Dialog footer buttons: Approve (disabled !isSourceSignable||isAllowanceLoading||hasAllowed||isApproving), Switch Chain (if isWrongChain), Swap (disabled !isSourceSignable||!hasAllowed||isSwapping||(Bitcoin dest && !isDestBitcoinReady)||nearStorage.blocksAction), plus conditional Request Trustline / Register Storage buttons. The DialogTrigger 'Swap' button onClick calls buildIntentParams().

ROUTE REGISTRATION (App.tsx diff): add `import SwapsApiPage from './pages/swaps-api/page';` near other page imports, and a child route object in the createBrowserRouter children array: `{ path: '/swaps-api', element: <SwapsApiPage /> }` (placed right after the /solver route).
NAV REGISTRATION (header.tsx diff): add `{ to: '/swaps-api', label: 'Swaps API' }` to the top-level `navLinks` array (between Solver and Bridge entries). navLinks entries are `{ to, label }`.

Shared utils the page reuses (already exist in working tree at apps/demo/src/lib/utils.ts): formatTokenAmount(amount, decimals, displayDecimals), calculateExchangeRate(amount: BigNumber, toAmount: BigNumber): BigNumber, formatMutationFailureMessage(error: unknown, fallback: string): string. And chainIdToChainName(chainId: SpokeChainKey): string in apps/demo/src/constants.ts.

On origin/feat/swaps-api-v2 the dapp-kit hooks live under packages/dapp-kit/src/hooks/swapsApi/ (each useSwapsApi* in its own file, plus index.ts barrel, isTerminalSwapIntentStatus.ts helper + test). The demo imports them all from the '@sodax/dapp-kit' barrel. Equivalent bridge-api hooks do NOT yet exist (no useBridgeApi* files on this branch) — they are the feature to be built by the dapp-kit dimension.

DIVISION OF RESPONSIBILITY confirmed: the demo page consumes ONLY (a) the useSwapsApi* API hooks (all calls carry apiConfig), (b) the local sign/broadcast dispatcher in lib/signAndBroadcast.ts, (c) wallet-layer hooks for account/provider/balance/switch-chain, and (d) minor local utils (mappers, config, debounce, SelectChain). No SDK business logic is reimplemented in the page except the Bitcoin BitcoinSpokeService.signAndSubmitRawTransaction direct call.
open_questions: Does Bridge need a quote step at all? A bridge is typically same-asset 1:1 across chains (possibly minus a fee); swaps-api quote returns quotedAmount for a different output token. The bridge-api page may drop useSwapsApiQuote/minOutputAmount/slippage or replace them with a fee/relay-fee display — needs the dapp-kit/SDK bridge-api hook surface to confirm.

Does Bridge have an approve/allowance step and a deadline? Swaps-api has useSwapsApiAllowance/useSwapsApiApprove/useSwapsApiDeadline. Whether the Bridge API exposes analogous endpoints (and whether createIntent returns the same { tx, intent, relayData } triple + a SubmitTxRequest with toIntentRequest mapping) must be verified against the new bridge-api hooks before mirroring.

Can lib/signAndBroadcast.ts, lib/mappers.ts, lib/useDebouncedValue.ts and SelectChain.tsx be shared/reused as-is rather than copied into a bridge-api folder? signAndBroadcast operates on the generic RawTxReturnType union and is feature-agnostic; mappers.ts is intent-shaped (IntentResponseV2->IntentRequestV2) and may need a bridge-specific variant if the bridge intent type differs.

Bitcoin handling: swaps-api routes Bitcoin source through BitcoinSpokeService and Bitcoin dest through the Bound trading wallet. Does the Bridge feature support Bitcoin, and if so does it reuse the same Bound path? Needs product confirmation.

Exact bridge-api hook names and param shapes (useBridgeApiTokens? useBridgeApiCreateIntent vs useBridgeApiBridge? submitTx request key 'request' vs 'body'?) are unknown on this branch — the bridge-api page recipe assumes a 1:1 naming mirror that the dapp-kit dimension must finalize.
replication_recipe: Create apps/demo/src/pages/bridge-api/page.tsx mirroring swaps-api/page.tsx: `const [orders, setOrders] = useState<BridgeApiOrder[]>([])`; render `orders.map(...) => <OrderStatus order={order}/>` then `<BridgeCard setOrders={setOrders}/>`. Export default BridgeApiPage. (Note the existing /bridge route stays; this is a new sibling /bridge-api route.)

Create apps/demo/src/components/bridge-api/lib/config.ts: export `BRIDGE_API_CONFIG = { baseURL: '<bridge canary base URL>' } as const satisfies RequestOverrideConfig` and pass it as `apiConfig` in EVERY bridge-api hook call (do not mutate app-wide SDK config).
Create apps/demo/src/components/bridge-api/lib/useDebouncedValue.ts — copy verbatim from swaps-api (or import the swaps-api one) for debouncing the amount input.

Create apps/demo/src/components/bridge-api/lib/signAndBroadcast.ts — copy the swaps-api dispatcher (signAndBroadcast<Feature>Tx + isSignable<Feature>Chain + waitForTxFinality + <Feature>SignError). It is feature-agnostic (operates on RawTxReturnType + IWalletProvider), so rename the symbols/error class for Bridge but keep the per-chain switch identical (EVM sendTransaction, SUI signAndExecuteTxn via {toJSON}, ICON sendTransaction, NEAR signAndSubmitTxn, SOLANA/STELLAR/STACKS/INJECTIVE signAndSendTransaction guarded, BITCOIN throws, waitForTxFinality EVM/ICON/SOLANA/STELLAR).

Create apps/demo/src/components/bridge-api/lib/mappers.ts — provide toIntentRequest (decimal-string IntentResponse -> bigint IntentRequest, BigInt-converting intentId/inputAmount/minOutputAmount/deadline/srcChain/dstChain) and toXToken (SwapTokenV2/BridgeTokenV2 -> branded XToken casting chainKey/hubAsset/vault), adapted to the bridge API's actual response/request types.
Create apps/demo/src/components/bridge-api/SelectChain.tsx — copy swaps-api SelectChain verbatim (generic over string[] chainList; chainDisplayName via chainIdToChainName try/catch).

Create apps/demo/src/components/bridge-api/OrderStatus.tsx — mirror swaps-api: define `BridgeApiOrder = { txHash: string; srcChainKey: string; apiBaseURL: string }`, build `apiConfig = useMemo(() => ({ baseURL: order.apiBaseURL }))`, poll via `useBridgeApiSubmitTxStatus({ params: { txHash, srcChainKey, apiConfig } })`, render status + result fields + failedAtStep/failureReason/userMessage.

Create apps/demo/src/components/bridge-api/BridgeCard.tsx mirroring SwapCard.tsx structure: (1) load tokens via useBridgeApiTokens({ params: { apiConfig } }) -> chainList=Object.keys; (2) src/dst chain+token state defaulting to ChainKeys constants, useEffect seeds default tokens; (3) wallet hooks useXAccount/useWalletProvider/useXService/useXBalances (convert tokens via toXToken) for balances + addresses; (4) IF bridge has a quote, debounce amount + useBridgeApiQuote, else skip; (5) buildIntentParams() resolving fresh deadline (useBridgeApiDeadline.refetch if present) and dstAddress; (6) allowance via useBridgeApiAllowance({ params: { body: intentParams, apiConfig } }); (7) handleApprove using useBridgeApiApprove().mutateAsyncSafe -> sign via signAndBroadcast -> waitForTxFinality -> refetchAllowance(); (8) handleSwap/handleBridge using useBridgeApiCreateIntent().mutateAsyncSafe -> destructure { tx, intent, relayData } -> sign+broadcast (Bitcoin special-case via sodax.spoke if supported) -> build SubmitTxRequest { txHash, srcChainKey, walletAddress, intent: toIntentRequest(intent), relayData: relayData.payload } -> useBridgeApiSubmitTx().mutateAsyncSafe -> on success setOrders([...prev, { txHash, srcChainKey, apiBaseURL: BRIDGE_API_CONFIG.baseURL }]) and close dialog.

Preserve the mutateAsyncSafe Result handling pattern in BridgeCard: every mutation returns `{ ok, value, error }`; on !ok call `setXError(formatMutationFailureMessage(result.error, '<fallback>'))` and return; never assume throw. Reuse existing apps/demo/src/lib/utils.ts helpers formatMutationFailureMessage / formatTokenAmount / calculateExchangeRate (no new utils needed).

Reuse shadcn UI primitives already in apps/demo/src/components/ui/ (Card, Dialog, Input, Label, Select, Button) and lucide icons; keep the Dialog-driven Approve/Switch-Chain/Bridge button layout with the same disabled-guard logic adapted to bridge (isSourceSignable, hasAllowed, isApproving, isBridging).

Register the route in apps/demo/src/App.tsx: add `import BridgeApiPage from './pages/bridge-api/page';` and a child route `{ path: '/bridge-api', element: <BridgeApiPage /> }` in the createBrowserRouter children array (next to the existing /bridge and /swaps-api routes).
Register the nav link in apps/demo/src/components/shared/header.tsx: add `{ to: '/bridge-api', label: 'Bridge API' }` to the navLinks array (e.g. right after the existing Bridge entry).

Carry over only the client-side prereqs that Bridge actually needs (Stellar trustline / NEAR storage / Bitcoin Bound wallet) — verify per-chain against the bridge feature's supported chains rather than copying all three blindly; each is independent UI+hook plumbing layered on the core API+sign+submit sequence.

Keep the demo page strictly a consumer: all bridge business logic must live in the new useBridgeApi* dapp-kit hooks + SDK; the page should only orchestrate hook calls, the local signAndBroadcast dispatcher, wallet-layer hooks, and minor mappers/config/debounce — matching the swaps-api separation.
